title: ouratea sellowii
intro: Ouratea sellowii, also known as the "Water Lily of the forest," is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae, native to South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Ouratea sellowii has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and is also used in the production of herbal supplements.
